template.name: WordPress by WP Zabbix Monitor
template.description:
Monitors a WordPress installation via the WP Zabbix Monitor plugin. Supports both HTTP Agent (pull) and Zabbix Sender (push) modes.
Required host macros: {$WP_URL} — WordPress site URL, e.g. https://example.com {$WP_API_TOKEN} — API token from the plugin settings page

macro.description: API token from WP Zabbix Monitor plugin settings
Memory usage warning threshold (% of limit)
DB query count warning threshold per request
Trigger when this many plugin updates are available
Trigger when this many cron events are overdue
WooCommerce: trigger when failed orders exceed this count
WooCommerce: trigger when pending orders exceed this count
WooCommerce: trigger when out-of-stock products exceed this count
WooCommerce: trigger when low-stock products exceed this count
WooCommerce: trigger when abandoned cart sessions exceed this count

item.name: WordPress: Raw metrics JSON
item.description: Master HTTP Agent item. All other items depend on this via JSONPath preprocessing.

item.name: WordPress: Site availability
item.description: 1 = site is reachable and plugin is active, 0 = unreachable.

item.name: WordPress: Raw metrics JSON (Push/Trapper)
item.description: Trapper master item for push mode. The WP plugin sends the full metrics JSON blob to this key via Zabbix Sender (zabbix_sender). Dependent items can use this as an alternative master alongside wordpress.metrics.raw (HTTP Agent pull mode).

trigger.name: WordPress: Site is unreachable
trigger.description: The WordPress REST API ping endpoint returned an error or the site is down.

trigger.name: WordPress: Page load time is critically high (avg > {$WP_LOAD_TIME_HIGH}ms)
trigger.description: Average page load time over 5 minutes exceeds the critical threshold.

trigger.name: WordPress: Page load time is elevated (avg > {$WP_LOAD_TIME_WARN}ms)

dependency.name: WordPress: Page load time is critically high (avg > {$WP_LOAD_TIME_HIGH}ms)

trigger.name: WordPress: Slow database queries detected
trigger.description: One or more database queries exceeded 50ms on the last page load.

trigger.name: WordPress: High database query count (> {$WP_DB_QUERY_WARN} per request)
WordPress: Disk usage is critically high (> {$WP_DISK_HIGH}%)

dependency.name: WordPress: Disk usage is critically high (> {$WP_DISK_HIGH}%)

trigger.name: WordPress: Plugin updates available ({ITEM.LASTVALUE} pending)
trigger.description: One or more plugins have updates available. Update promptly to maintain security.

trigger.name: WordPress: Cron events are overdue ({ITEM.LASTVALUE} events)
trigger.description: WP-Cron has overdue events. This may indicate the cron system is not running correctly.

trigger.name: WordPress: WP_DEBUG is enabled in production
trigger.description: WP_DEBUG is enabled. This should be disabled on production sites to prevent information disclosure.

trigger.name: WordPress: PHP OPcache is disabled
trigger.description: OPcache is not enabled. Enabling OPcache significantly improves WordPress performance.

trigger.name: WooCommerce: High number of failed orders ({ITEM.LASTVALUE})
trigger.description: The number of failed orders has exceeded the warning threshold. This may indicate a payment gateway issue.

trigger.name: WooCommerce: High number of pending orders ({ITEM.LASTVALUE})
trigger.description: Pending orders have exceeded the warning threshold. Orders may be stuck awaiting payment confirmation.

trigger.name: WooCommerce: Multiple products out of stock ({ITEM.LASTVALUE})
trigger.description: The number of out-of-stock products has exceeded the warning threshold.

trigger.name: WooCommerce: Multiple products below low-stock threshold ({ITEM.LASTVALUE})
trigger.description: Several products are running low on stock and may need restocking soon.

trigger.name: WooCommerce: High abandoned cart count ({ITEM.LASTVALUE} sessions)
trigger.description: A large number of sessions have items in the cart but have not completed checkout in the last 24 hours.
